title: Chicken Vindaloo (Goan-Style)

ingredients.title: For the Vindaloo Masala Paste
items: 3 tbsp oil (or a mix of oil + ghee for richness)
2 tbsp grated fresh coconut (optional but adds subtle creaminess)

instruction.title: 1. Marinate the Chicken
steps: Mix chicken with vinegar, turmeric, red chili powder, and salt.
Marinate at least 2–3 hours, ideally overnight for maximum flavor penetration.

instruction.title: 2. Caramelize Onions
steps: Heat oil/ghee in a heavy pan.
Add sliced onions and cook on medium-low heat until deep golden brown (this can take 15–20 minutes).
Stir often to prevent burning; caramelized onions give richness and natural sweetness.

instruction.title: 3. Prepare the Masala Paste
steps: Dry roast cumin, coriander, black pepper, fennel seeds, cloves, cardamom, and cinnamon until aromatic.
Grind roasted spices with chopped tomatoes, grated coconut, and 2 tbsp water to form a smooth paste.

instruction.title: 4. Cook the Chicken
steps: Add ginger-garlic paste to caramelized onions, sauté 1–2 minutes.
Stir in the masala paste, cook for 5–7 minutes until oil separates from the paste.
Add marinated chicken, sear lightly for 5–7 minutes.
Pour in water or stock to desired consistency. Cover and simmer on low heat for 25–30 minutes until chicken is tender.

instruction.title: 5. Finish
steps: Taste and adjust salt, spice, and vinegar. Add sugar if needed to balance tang.
Garnish generously with chopped coriander leaves.
Serve hot with steamed rice, Goan sannas, or soft parathas.

chefTips: Caramelized onions + coconut: gives a deep, luxurious flavor without heaviness.
Vinegar punch: Goan-style vindaloo always has a sharp but balanced tang.
Low, slow simmer: ensures chicken stays juicy and absorbs the masala fully.
Optional finishing oil drizzle: a teaspoon of ghee on top just before serving adds that restaurant finish.
